summarization.a: The figure displays an XPS chart of the Mo 3d doublet of MoOx with the results of three samples superimposed.

data_extraction.a:
| Sample | binding energy (eV) |
|---|---|
| 2 second O2 | Peaks at 232.5 and 235.5 |
| 2 second O2 & Ar | Peaks at 232.5 and 235.5 |
| 1 second O2 | Peaks at 232.5 and 235.5 |

a.question: What does the peak at 232.5 eV correspond to?
What does the peak at 235.6 eV correspond to?
What is the ratio of the areas of the peak at 232.5 eV to the one at 235.6 eV?
Do the superimposed spectra differ from each other? What is the implication of this?
a.answer: No, they do not differ. This means that the oxidation state of Mo and its chemical environment are the same.
